"Sec. 1  
[The equation involves a fourth-degree polynomial and an absolute value term. To determine the number of real solutions, we can analyze the behavior of the function on both sides of the equation. The left-hand side is a continuous function, and its graph will help us understand how many times it intersects with the constant value on the right-hand side.]

Sec. 2  
[By examining the function for positive and negative values of x, we find that the equation has a total of four real solutions. The symmetry of the absolute value term and the even degree of the polynomial contribute to this result.]"
